 {"id":"bd-2b3","title":"Fix potential integer overflow in buffer size calculations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:36:10.348376332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:33.940342789Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:33.940281014Z","close_reason":"Fixed integer overflow in renderer total_cells calculation using saturating_mul","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2bnv","title":"demo_showcase tests: capability + size degradation matrix (headless snapshots)","description":"# demo_showcase Tests — Capability + Size Degradation Matrix (Headless Snapshots)

## Purpose

`bd-1ob` defines resilience rules and capability gating. We need regression coverage that proves:

- the demo stays usable across constrained terminals
- the *same* constraints continue to work as the demo evolves

This bead adds a matrix of deterministic headless runs that snapshot the demo’s JSON dump across:

- multiple terminal sizes (including "too small" -> compact layout)
- multiple capability presets (no truecolor / no hyperlinks / no mouse / minimal)

## Prerequisites / Contract

This bead assumes headless mode supports:

- `--headless-dump-json`
- `--headless-size <WxH>` (or equivalent) to force a specific buffer size
- `--cap-preset <name>` (or equivalent) to force effective capability flags used by gating

The JSON output must include (at minimum):

- effective capabilities actually used by the demo (after overrides)
- selected layout mode (full vs compact)
- a small set of sentinel markers (panel headers, mode, warnings)

## Matrix (Initial)

Run and snapshot these scenarios (exact names can evolve, but keep the set stable):

1) `ideal_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: ideal/auto

2) `no_truecolor_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: no_truecolor

3) `no_hyperlinks_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: no_hyperlinks

4) `no_mouse_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: no_mouse

5) `minimal_80x24`
   - size: 80x24
   - cap preset: minimal

6) `tiny_50x15`
   - size: 50x15
   - cap preset: minimal
   - expectation: compact layout triggers

## Assertions (Beyond Snapshots)

For each scenario, assert simple invariants in addition to insta snapshots:

- process exits 0
- JSON includes `warnings` when capabilities are missing
- `tiny_50x15` reports compact layout active
- hyperlink markers are absent when cap preset disables hyperlinks

## Logging / Artifacts

On failure:

- print exact reproduction command
- store JSON and stdout/stderr as artifacts when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`

## Why This Matters

Capability regressions are the most common way for demos to feel "broken" on someone else’s machine.
This matrix makes resilience an enforced property.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- Integration tests run the defined headless matrix scenarios (size + cap preset) and exit 0 for each.
- Each scenario’s JSON dump is snapshotted with insta and remains deterministic across runs.
- Tests include extra invariants beyond snapshots (e.g., `tiny_50x15` reports compact layout; OSC 8 absent when hyperlinks disabled).
- On failure, logs include reproduction commands and artifacts are written when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.188432073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:19.403799663Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","resilience","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.213975224Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.217677885Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-2el","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.210212961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.206280571Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2c6","title":"demo_showcase UI: logs panel (event stream + hyperlink styling + scroll)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Logs Panel (Events + Hyperlinks)

## Purpose

Logs panel provides:

- “real app” texture
- proof of styled text + colors
- proof of hyperlinks via OSC 8 (`LinkPool`)
- proof of scroll + scissor clipping

## Content

- Deterministic log stream derived from time
- Levels: INFO/WARN/ERROR with distinct styling
- A few special rows that contain a URL and are rendered as a hyperlink

## Hyperlink Strategy

- Allocate URL ids from `renderer.link_pool()`
- Apply `Style::fg(...).with_underline().with_link(link_id)` to the cell range

Note: terminals handle click-to-open; we still render pressed/hover state visually.

## Interaction

- Scroll wheel scrolls logs when hovered
- Click a row to select it
- Selected row shows details in a small “inspector” area (or toast)","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Logs render with level styling and timestamps
- [ ] Panel scrolls and clips correctly
- [ ] At least one OSC 8 hyperlink is rendered
- [ ] Clicking a row selects it (HitGrid)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.206857206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:28.336410055Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:56.725549700Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:57.283113630Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-3ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:19.154424991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.233835087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.195606978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:28.336381372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2el","title":"demo_showcase runtime: headless smoke mode (no TTY, render-to-memory, exit 0)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Headless Smoke Mode (No TTY, Render-to-Memory, Exit 0)

## Purpose

We want a CI-friendly way to validate that the demo binary:

- compiles
- can construct its app state
- can execute its render pipeline

…without requiring a real terminal.

This enables deterministic tests, fast iteration, and regression snapshots.

## Behavior

Primary flag:

- --headless-smoke

When enabled:

- do not enter alt screen
- do not enable raw mode
- do not attempt mouse tracking
- build App + DemoContent
- render into an in-memory OptimizedBuffer
- exercise diff computation between frames (via BufferDiff::compute) to prove the diff path
- exit 0 if all frames render without panic

## Frame Count / Termination Rules

There are two headless execution modes:

### A) Plain headless smoke (no tour)

- default: run a small fixed N frames (fast in CI)
- if `--max-frames <N>` is provided, use that as the frame count

### B) Headless tour run (`--tour`)

- default: run until the tour script completes, then exit 0
  - (implementation can be "fast" by advancing time deterministically without sleeping)
- if `--max-frames <N>` is provided, treat it as a hard safety bound:
  - exit 0 only if tour completed within N frames
  - otherwise exit non-zero with a clear error

This avoids forcing tests to guess the number of frames required to finish a tour.

## Size + Capability Control (For Matrix Testing)

- `--headless-size <WxH>` forces the headless buffer size (default 80x24)
- `--cap-preset <name>` defines the effective capabilities used by gating and rendering decisions

## Tour Integration

- If --tour is set, headless mode executes the tour script deterministically.
- Tour step markers must be included in the JSON dump.

## Output Contract (For Tests)

Headless mode must print a stable, parseable summary line to stdout so integration tests can assert success without guessing:

- HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK frames=<N> last_dirty_cells=<K>

Structured dump (recommended for deeper tests):

- --headless-dump-json

When enabled, print JSON to stdout containing (minimum):

- config summary (theme, fps cap, seed, flags)
- effective capabilities used (after cap preset / gating)
- headless size used (WxH)
- selected layout mode (full vs compact)
- warnings list (e.g., "no_truecolor", "no_mouse", "no_hyperlinks")
- per-frame stats (dirty cell count, dt)
- sentinel markers proving panels rendered (top bar title, panel headers, current section)
- tour step markers (if tour enabled)

Avoid dumping the full buffer unless explicitly requested (too large for CI logs).

## Implementation Notes

This likely requires the demo’s render pipeline to be factored into a pure function:

- fn render_frame(app, layout, buffer, pool, links, now)

Renderer I/O should be a thin wrapper around that.

## Acceptance Notes

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke` works in non-TTY CI environments.
- Results are deterministic across runs.
- Output contains HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK on success.
- JSON schema includes the fields required by `bd-2649` and `bd-2bnv`.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke` works in non-TTY CI environments and exits 0.
- Success output includes `HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK` with stable key/value fields.
- With `--headless-dump-json`, stdout includes valid JSON containing: effective caps, headless size, layout mode, warnings, and sentinel markers.
- `--headless-size` and `--cap-preset` change the effective caps/size reflected in JSON (enabling matrix snapshots).
- With `--tour`, headless execution completes the tour and exits 0 by default; `--max-frames` can bound runtime and fails clearly if too low.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:05.942661331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:45.330916901Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:05.964184936Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:12.868058851Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:14.567529855Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:14.001649143Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:13.424913256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Purpose

- compiles

This enables deterministic tests, fast iteration, and regression snapshots.

## Behavior

When enabled:

## Frame Count / Termination Rules

There are two headless execution modes:

### A) Plain headless smoke (no tour)

### B) Headless tour run (`--tour`)

## Size + Capability Control (For Matrix Testing)

- `--cap-preset <name>` defines the effective capabilities used by gating and rendering decisions

Renderer I/O should be a thin wrapper around that.

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke` works in non-TTY CI environments.
- Results are deterministic across runs.
- Output contains HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK on success.
- JSON schema includes the fields required by `bd-2649` and `bd-2bnv`.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:

- `--headless-size` and `--cap-preset` change the effective caps/size reflected in JSON (enabling matrix snapshots).
- With `--tour`, headless execution completes the tour and exits 0 by default; `--max-frames` can bound runtime and fails clearly if too low.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:05.942661331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:24:11.107705860Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:05.964184936Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:12.868058851Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:14.567529855Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-3vjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:24:11.107678429Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:14.001649143Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:13.424913256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ib","title":"Improve render_to/render_to_with_pool documentation","description":"The render_to() method creates placeholders for multi-codepoint graphemes when no pool is available. See test test_render_emoji_produces_placeholder and test_render_emoji_with_pool.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:40:47.761765989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:45.133867508Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:45.133807647Z","close_reason":"Documentation already in place - render_to() and render_to_with_pool() both have comprehensive doc comments explaining grapheme handling behavior, when to use each method, and includes a code example.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-2iv","title":"demo_showcase runtime: CLI args + Config (modes, toggles, fps cap)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — CLI Args + Config

## Purpose

Provide a minimal, dependency-free CLI surface so the demo is easy to run in different modes (interactive, tour, headless testing) while staying deterministic.

## Constraints

- No new deps for CLI parsing.
- Keep parsing strict and predictable.
- Default behavior must be deterministic (no time-based randomness).

## Proposed Flags

Core interactive flags:

- --help: print usage
- --tour: start tour mode immediately
- --fps <N>: cap FPS (sleep to maintain)
- --no-mouse: don’t enable mouse tracking
- --no-alt-screen: don’t enter alt screen
- --no-cap-queries: skip capability queries

Deterministic termination controls (critical for E2E + recordings):

- --max-frames <N>: hard frame budget; exit 0 after presenting N frames
- --exit-after-tour: when tour reaches the last step, exit 0 automatically

Headless/testing flags:

- --headless-smoke: run a deterministic headless render sequence and exit
- --headless-dump-json: dump structured JSON (for snapshots/regressions)
- --headless-check <name>: run a specific deterministic check and exit (layout/palette/hitgrid…)
- --headless-size <WxH>: force the headless buffer size (default 80x24)

Capability override (for matrix tests + debugging):

- --cap-preset <name>
  - allowed (initial): auto | ideal | no_truecolor | no_hyperlinks | no_mouse | minimal
  - semantics:
    - interactive (TTY): preset can only DISABLE capabilities (effective = detected ∩ preset)
    - headless: preset defines the effective capabilities directly

Optional/advanced:

- --seed <N>: deterministic parameterization for animations (optional)
- --threaded: use ThreadedRenderer backend (optional)

## Determinism Rules

- Default seed is a fixed constant (e.g., 0).
- Never default to using time for seed.
- Tour mode must be deterministic regardless of seed (either force seed=0, or make tour steps independent of seed).

## Config Shape

Example:

struct Config {
    start_in_tour: bool,
    fps_cap: Option<u32>,
    enable_mouse: bool,
    use_alt_screen: bool,
    query_capabilities: bool,

    // Deterministic termination
    max_frames: Option<u64>,
    exit_after_tour: bool,

    // Headless/test
    headless_smoke: bool,
    headless_dump_json: bool,
    headless_check: Option<String>,
    headless_size: Option<(u16, u16)>,

    // Capability override
    cap_preset: Option<CapPreset>,

    // Advanced
    threaded: bool,
    seed: u64,
}

impl Config {
    fn renderer_options(&self) -> RendererOptions { ... }
}

## Notes

- Run-loop semantics for `--max-frames` and `--exit-after-tour` are tracked in `bd-3vjm`.
- The primary consumer of `--cap-preset` + `--headless-size` is the degradation matrix suite in `bd-2bnv`.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] `demo_showcase --help` prints usage
- [ ] Flags parsed without external deps
- [ ] `Config` cleanly maps into `RendererOptions`
- [ ] `--headless-smoke` works without a TTY","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:45.760572467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:19.726389729Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:14.034409174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:45.773427812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:42.537129324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2iv","title":"demo_showcase runtime: CLI args + Config (modes, toggles, fps cap)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — CLI Args + Config

## Purpose

Provide a minimal, dependency-free CLI surface so the demo is easy to run in different modes (interactive, tour, headless testing) while staying deterministic.

## Constraints

- No new deps for CLI parsing.
- Keep parsing strict and predictable.
- Default behavior must be deterministic (no time-based randomness).

## Proposed Flags

Core interactive flags:

- --help: print usage
- --tour: start tour mode immediately
- --fps <N>: cap FPS (sleep to maintain)
- --no-mouse: don’t enable mouse tracking
- --no-alt-screen: don’t enter alt screen
- --no-cap-queries: skip capability queries

Deterministic termination controls (critical for E2E + recordings):

- --max-frames <N>: hard frame budget; exit 0 after presenting N frames
- --exit-after-tour: when tour reaches the last step, exit 0 automatically

Headless/testing flags:

- --headless-smoke: run a deterministic headless render sequence and exit
- --headless-dump-json: dump structured JSON (for snapshots/regressions)
- --headless-check <name>: run a specific deterministic check and exit (layout/palette/hitgrid…)
- --headless-size <WxH>: force the headless buffer size (default 80x24)
  - parsing: accept `80x24` (lowercase x), reject ambiguous formats

Capability override (for matrix tests + debugging):

- --cap-preset <name>
  - allowed (initial): auto | ideal | no_truecolor | no_hyperlinks | no_mouse | minimal
  - semantics:
    - interactive (TTY): presets can only DISABLE capabilities (effective = detected ∩ preset)
      - NOTE: `ideal` behaves like `auto` in interactive mode (cannot force-enable terminal features).
    - headless: preset defines the effective capabilities directly

Optional/advanced:

- --seed <N>: deterministic parameterization for animations (optional)
- --threaded: use ThreadedRenderer backend (optional)

## Determinism Rules

- Default seed is a fixed constant (e.g., 0).
- Never default to using time for seed.
- Tour mode must be deterministic regardless of seed (either force seed=0, or make tour steps independent of seed).

## Config Shape

Example:

struct Config {
    start_in_tour: bool,
    fps_cap: Option<u32>,
    enable_mouse: bool,
    use_alt_screen: bool,
    query_capabilities: bool,

    // Deterministic termination
    max_frames: Option<u64>,
    exit_after_tour: bool,

    // Headless/test
    headless_smoke: bool,
    headless_dump_json: bool,
    headless_check: Option<String>,
    headless_size: Option<(u16, u16)>,

    // Capability override
    cap_preset: Option<CapPreset>,

    // Advanced
    threaded: bool,
    seed: u64,
}

impl Config {
    fn renderer_options(&self) -> RendererOptions { ... }
}

## Notes

- Run-loop semantics for `--max-frames` and `--exit-after-tour` are tracked in `bd-3vjm`.
- The primary consumer of `--cap-preset` + `--headless-size` is the degradation matrix suite in `bd-2bnv`.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] `demo_showcase --help` prints usage
- [ ] Flags parsed without external deps
- [ ] `Config` cleanly maps into `RendererOptions`
- [ ] `--headless-smoke` works without a TTY","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:45.760572467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:24:52.007619136Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:14.034409174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:45.773427812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:42.537129324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jc","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: product spec, UX, and tour script","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Product Spec, UX, and “Tour Mode” Script

## Purpose

Before we write a large flagship demo, we lock a **cohesive product story** so implementation doesn’t devolve into a random pile of feature snippets.

This epic defines:

- The *app concept* (what kind of “realistic app” this is pretending to be)
- The information architecture (panels, navigation, overlays)
- The interaction model (keyboard + mouse, focus rules)
- The visual design system (palette, typography, spacing)
- A deterministic **tour script** that can auto-drive the UI (for demos/videos)

## Constraints (From Project Philosophy)

- OpenTUI is a **rendering engine**, not a framework. This demo must not introduce a widget tree/layout system into the library.
- The demo should rely on OpenTUI primitives: buffers, cells, styles, scissor/opacity, diff rendering.

## Outputs

- A detailed spec embedded directly into the child beads under this epic.
- A stable keybinding map and “tour steps” list that other beads can reference.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] The demo’s screens/panels are explicitly defined (what appears where)
- [ ] Keybindings and mouse behaviors are explicitly defined
- [ ] A deterministic tour script is specified (step-by-step)
- [ ] A visual design system (colors/styles) is specified
- [ ] Clear success criteria for “polish” are defined (what good looks like)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:03:43.707648876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:03:55.305694849Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","epic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jc","depends_on_id":"bd-1co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:03:43.727602281Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kgg","title":"demo_showcase features: diff rendering + sync output prove-out (stats + invalidate)","description":"# demo_showcase Features — Diff Rendering + Sync Output Prove‑Out (Stats + Invalidate)

## Purpose

OpenTUI’s biggest performance claim is diff-based rendering (only changed cells output) and synchronized output to eliminate flicker.

This bead ensures the demo makes that visible.

## Requirements

- The inspector/perf panel must show:
  - `cells updated` last frame
  - an estimate of bytes written (if available)
  - whether sync output is active

- Provide a UI control or keybind to:
  - force a full redraw (`Renderer::invalidate()` then `present()`)
  - toggle the debug overlay

- In tour mode, include a step that:
  - triggers a full redraw
  - then returns to normal diff mode

## Acceptance Notes

- Viewer can see “small updates” vs “full redraw” reflected in stats.
- Sync output capability is reported accurately and is enabled when supported.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- The inspector/perf UI shows per-frame changed cells and indicates whether sync output is active.
- A user control/keybind triggers a forced full redraw (`invalidate` + present) and the stats visibly reflect it.
- A user control/keybind toggles the debug overlay.
- Tour mode includes a step that triggers a full redraw and then returns to normal diff mode.
- Sync output reporting is accurate and is enabled when supported.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:51.600548455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:22.452164121Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kgg","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.860276835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kgg","depends_on_id":"bd-37p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:51.615070887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kgg","depends_on_id":"bd-pnln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:19.205239933Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kgg","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:31.884378092Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Focus on doc comments with examples.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:37.918848027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:39:34.372249544Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:39:34.372203978Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive documentation to core public API types (Rgba, Cell, Style, OptimizedBuffer, Renderer) with module-level docs, struct-level docs, and 6 runnable doctest examples.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3ot0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: optional ThreadedRenderer backend (--threaded)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Optional ThreadedRenderer Backend (`--threaded`)

## Purpose

OpenTUI has a `ThreadedRenderer` that moves terminal I/O off the main thread. The flagship demo should be able to showcase this as an optional mode.

This is not required for the first “works and looks amazing” milestone, but it’s an impressive proof point for real applications.

## Behavior

- CLI flag: `--threaded`
- When enabled, the demo uses `opentui::renderer::ThreadedRenderer` instead of `Renderer`.
- The UI + state machine should behave the same.

## Design Constraint

`Renderer` and `ThreadedRenderer` have different ownership models (threaded moves buffers/pools through channels). To avoid duplicating the whole app:

- Introduce a small demo-only backend wrapper:
  - `enum Backend { Direct(Renderer), Threaded(ThreadedRenderer) }`
  - helper methods: `buffer()`, `present()`, `resize()`, `set_title()`, `set_cursor()`

## GraphemePool Note (Important)

If tour/unicode panels use grapheme pool content, ensure that the threaded path does not leak grapheme refs:

- Avoid using `ThreadedRenderer::clear()` if it doesn’t clear with pool.
- Prefer explicit `buffer().clear_with_pool(grapheme_pool(), bg)` / `resize_with_pool` patterns.
- If the library needs a small fix (pool-aware clear/resize convenience), file a follow-up bead or patch the library directly.

## Acceptance Notes

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` works and exits cleanly.
- Rendering remains smooth and input remains responsive.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` runs the full app using `ThreadedRenderer` and behaves like the direct backend.
- Input remains responsive and rendering remains smooth.
- Grapheme pool usage is safe in the threaded path (no leaked refs; correct pool-aware clear/resize semantics).
- The app exits cleanly and restores terminal state.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.107585716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:19:34.059502793Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.142106801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.844522494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962802535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.396568255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pe","title":"demo_showcase UI: help overlay (F1) + feature legend","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Help Overlay (F1) + Feature Legend

## Purpose

The help overlay makes the demo self-contained and also proves multiple library features:

- overlay rendering + opacity stack (glass modal)
- scissor clipping (scrollable help content)
- hyperlink rendering via OSC 8 (LinkPool)

It also prevents the “what do I press?” problem for first-time viewers.

## Content Requirements

### Keybinds

Include (at minimum):

- `Ctrl+Q` quit
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` focus next/prev panel
- `Ctrl+P` command palette
- `F1` help
- `Ctrl+T` tour start/stop
- editor keys (arrows, word movement, wrap toggle, undo/redo)
- log/preview navigation keys

### Mouse

- click to focus panels
- click to activate buttons
- scroll to scroll lists/logs

### Feature Legend

Make it explicit where each OpenTUI feature is visible in the app:

- RGBA alpha blending (preview overlays / glass modals)
- scissor stack (sidebar/log scroll clipping)
- opacity stack (overlays + tinted UI sections)
- grapheme pool (unicode panel)
- OSC 8 links (logs + help)
- hitgrid (clickable UI)
- pixel buffers (preview panel)
- diff rendering + sync output (perf/inspector + smoothness)

### Clickable Links

Include a small “Docs / Links” section with OSC 8 hyperlinks:

- repo URL
- unicode width reference

The links must be allocated through `renderer.link_pool()` and applied via `Style::with_link`.

## Rendering Notes

- Help content should be scrollable when terminal is small.
- Use clear typography hierarchy: title, section headers, monospace keycaps.
- Use hit areas for the “Close” button and for any tab-like subviews.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- F1 toggles a help overlay that clearly explains keybinds, mouse interactions, and where each OpenTUI feature is demonstrated.
- Help content is scrollable and clipped via scissor when space is limited.
- Help overlay includes OSC 8 hyperlinks (when supported) allocated via `renderer.link_pool()`.
- Close controls work via both keyboard and HitGrid.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.642163943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:19:27.570230907Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:04.565909821Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666002312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.092958035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.656207314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vjm","title":"demo_showcase runtime: deterministic termination controls (--max-frames / --exit-after-tour)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Deterministic Termination Controls

## Purpose

We need deterministic ways to stop the app without human input so we can:

- run real PTY-based E2E tests without brittle "send keys" logic
- record unattended videos reliably (tour ends -> app exits)
- keep runs bounded for CI and debugging

## Required Controls

### 1) `--max-frames <N>` (Hard Frame Budget)

- After the demo has **presented N frames**, exit 0.
- Count frames consistently across modes (interactive + headless).
- Print a stable summary line on exit (useful for tests/logs), e.g.:
  - `EXIT_OK reason=max_frames frames=<N> last_dirty_cells=<K>`

### 2) `--exit-after-tour` (Tour Completion Exit)

- When tour mode reaches the last step and completes it, exit 0 automatically.
- The exit should be clean:
  - restore cursor
  - leave alt screen (if used)
  - disable mouse (if enabled)
- Tour should still be escapable with user input; this flag only adds an automatic exit at completion.

## Integration Notes

- Flag parsing belongs in `bd-2iv`, but the **run-loop semantics** and **tour end handling** live here.
- Headless mode (`bd-2el`) should also honor these controls so tests can bound runtime.

## Why This Matters

Without deterministic termination, we end up with flaky tests and awkward demo workflows.
These controls make the demo feel "professional" to develop and validate.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `--max-frames <N>` exits 0 after presenting N frames in interactive mode and in headless smoke mode.
- `--exit-after-tour` exits 0 when the final tour step completes (both interactive and headless tour runs).
- Exits are clean: cursor restored, mouse disabled (if enabled), and alt screen exited (if used).
- A stable summary line is printed on exit for deterministic debugging/logging.
- PTY E2E tests can rely on these controls to avoid sending synthetic keypresses.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.678047128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:26.318650041Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.707235213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.718517533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.723946582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.712843797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-61g0","title":"demo_showcase e2e: PTY harness + ANSI capture (real terminal sequences, ignored by default)","description":"# demo_showcase E2E — PTY Harness + ANSI Capture (Ignored by Default)

## Purpose

Headless tests prove the render pipeline is correct, but they cannot prove real terminal I/O behavior.

This bead adds true end-to-end tests by running the demo under a pseudo-terminal (PTY), capturing ANSI output bytes, and asserting on key invariants:

- alternate screen entry/exit
- cursor hide/show
- mouse enable sequences (if enabled)
- synchronized output enable (if supported)
- OSC 8 hyperlink sequences appear (and do NOT appear when disabled)

## Approach (Unix)

- Use libc openpty / fork+exec (or an equivalent minimal PTY approach) to spawn:
  - cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames 600 --fps 30

Why both exit controls?

- `--exit-after-tour` is the intended deterministic terminator.
- `--max-frames` is a hard safety bound so the PTY test cannot hang if tour logic regresses.

- Capture stdout from the PTY master.

## Assertions

Assert the output stream contains, in some order:

- enter alt screen (CSI ? 1049 h) when alt screen enabled
- leave alt screen (CSI ? 1049 l) on exit
- hide cursor (CSI ? 25 l) and show cursor (CSI ? 25 h)
- mouse enable sequences (1000/1002/1006) when mouse enabled
- synchronized output enable (CSI ? 2026 h) when supported/enabled

Hyperlink coverage:

- run one case with default settings and assert at least one OSC 8 sequence appears (ESC ] 8 ; ; ...)
- run one case with `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks` (or equivalent disable) and assert OSC 8 sequences are absent

Optional (high-value): threaded mode parity

- run a second spawn with `--threaded` and assert the same terminal lifecycle sequences appear

Also assert:

- demo exits 0 within a timeout
- output is non-trivial (bytes > some threshold)

## Logging / Artifacts

- Use the structured logger utilities in `tests/common/harness.rs` to:
  - log key milestones (spawned, bytes captured, exit status)
  - write captured ANSI bytes to artifacts (both raw + hex/escaped view)

Controlled via HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1.

## Test Placement

- Mark PTY tests as #[ignore] by default to avoid CI flakiness.
- Provide a script (separate bead) to run them locally and collect artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- An ignored PTY-based test suite can spawn `demo_showcase` with `--tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames` and capture a non-trivial ANSI output stream.
- Assertions cover: alt screen enter/exit, cursor hide/show, mouse enable (when enabled), and sync output enable (when supported/enabled).
- Hyperlink behavior is covered: OSC 8 sequences present by default and absent under `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks`.
- Optional: a threaded run case (`--threaded`) also passes the same terminal lifecycle assertions.
- On failure, artifacts include raw and escaped/hex output when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.613268330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:54.313815556Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.862668126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.631306455Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:28.902157577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3vjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:19.691230196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:26.694765952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-61g0","title":"demo_showcase e2e: PTY harness + ANSI capture (real terminal sequences, ignored by default)","description":"# demo_showcase E2E — PTY Harness + ANSI Capture (Ignored by Default)

## Purpose

Headless tests prove the render pipeline is correct, but they cannot prove real terminal I/O behavior.

This bead adds true end-to-end tests by running the demo under a pseudo-terminal (PTY), capturing ANSI output bytes, and asserting on key invariants:

- alternate screen entry/exit
- cursor hide/show
- mouse enable sequences (if enabled)
- synchronized output enable (if supported)
- OSC 8 hyperlink sequences appear (and do NOT appear when disabled)

## Approach (Unix)

- Use libc openpty / fork+exec (or an equivalent minimal PTY approach) to spawn:
  - cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames 600 --fps 30

Why both exit controls?

- `--exit-after-tour` is the intended deterministic terminator.
- `--max-frames` is a hard safety bound so the PTY test cannot hang if tour logic regresses.

- Capture stdout from the PTY master.

## Assertions

Assert the output stream contains, in some order:

- enter alt screen (CSI ? 1049 h) when alt screen enabled
- leave alt screen (CSI ? 1049 l) on exit
- hide cursor (CSI ? 25 l) and show cursor (CSI ? 25 h)
- mouse enable sequences (1000/1002/1006) when mouse enabled
- synchronized output enable (CSI ? 2026 h) when supported/enabled

Hyperlink coverage:

- run one case with default settings and assert at least one OSC 8 sequence appears (ESC ] 8 ; ; ...)
- run one case with `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks` (or equivalent disable) and assert OSC 8 sequences are absent

Optional (high-value): threaded mode parity

- run a second spawn with `--threaded` and assert the same terminal lifecycle sequences appear

Also assert:

- demo exits 0 within a timeout
- output is non-trivial (bytes > some threshold)

## Logging / Artifacts

When the test runs, log (to stdout/stderr, and to artifacts when enabled):

- full spawn command (argv)
- exit status + duration
- bytes captured
- TERM + locale info (if set) and any environment overrides used

Artifacts (controlled via HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1):

- captured ANSI bytes (raw)
- escaped/hex view for grep-friendly debugging
- stdout/stderr from the child process

Reuse the existing harness conventions:

- HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1
- HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR=target/test-artifacts

## Test Placement

- Mark PTY tests as #[ignore] by default to avoid CI flakiness.
- Provide a script (separate bead) to run them locally and collect artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- An ignored PTY-based test suite can spawn `demo_showcase` with `--tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames` and capture a non-trivial ANSI output stream.
- Assertions cover: alt screen enter/exit, cursor hide/show, mouse enable (when enabled), and sync output enable (when supported/enabled).
- Hyperlink behavior is covered: OSC 8 sequences present by default and absent under `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks`.
- Optional: a threaded run case (`--threaded`) also passes the same terminal lifecycle assertions.
- On failure, artifacts include raw and escaped/hex output when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.613268330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:25:12.630497260Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:23:05.470334996Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.862668126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.631306455Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:28.902157577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3vjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:19.691230196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:26.694765952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bqd1","title":"demo_showcase tests: tour determinism regression (scripted steps -> stable output)","description":"# demo_showcase Tests — Tour Determinism Regression (Scripted Steps → Stable Output)

## Purpose

Tour mode is a flagship feature and also our best deterministic “integration test” for:

- input routing
- overlays
- panel switching
- pixel/alpha/unicode/link features

This bead adds regression tests that ensure the tour produces stable, deterministic outputs.

## Strategy

Run the demo in headless mode and execute the tour script (no real TTY).

Recommended CLI shape (keep it strict):

- --headless-smoke --tour --headless-dump-json

Optional safety bound (should not be needed if tour completes, but prevents hangs):

- --max-frames <N>

The JSON output must include:

- step index + step title
- per-frame (or per-step) dirty cell counts
- sentinel markers proving that step’s feature is active

## What To Assert

For each tour step (from `bd-1gy`):

- the expected step title appears in the JSON
- the expected feature markers are present, e.g.:
  - palette open marker on the command palette step
  - unicode strings (ZWJ emoji) on the grapheme pool step
  - at least one OSC8 link marker on the hyperlinks step
  - pixel preview active marker on the pixel step

Additionally:

- tour output is deterministic across runs (snapshot the JSON via insta or compare stable hashes)
- tour terminates cleanly (headless tour run exits 0)

## Logging

- Print a clear reproduction command on failure.
- Store JSON and logs as artifacts when HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1.

## Acceptance Notes

- A CI test fails loudly if tour output changes unexpectedly.
- The logged diff is sufficient to diagnose what changed.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- A CI-friendly test runs `demo_showcase` headlessly with `--tour --headless-dump-json` and exits 0.
- The resulting JSON includes step markers and feature markers per step.
- The test asserts determinism via insta snapshots (or stable hashes) across runs.
- On failure, logs include reproduction commands and artifacts are stored when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:31.832960799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:00.579873891Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-2el","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:53.529196779Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:31.852222684Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bqd1","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:52.933298797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
